extra.reasoning: The input variable `vomiting` does not appear in the condition of any rule (r1-r10). The rule conditions reference, respectively: severe_headache, stiff_neck, pallor, sweating, hemoptysis, dizziness, vision_loss, back_pain, chest_pain, slurred_speech. None mention `vomiting`. Because `vomiting` is never read by any rule (nor by the default), toggling it from false to true while holding all other inputs fixed cannot change which rules match, cannot change priority/tie-break resolution, and cannot change whether the default applies. Hence the resulting `acuity` is identical before and after setting `vomiting` true for every possible assignment of the other inputs. "Identical" satisfies "no lower than before" (equal is not lower). Therefore the monotonicity property holds across the entire input space; no counterexample exists.
